title: Black-Scholes and the Greeks — SuperBEST v4 Exact Node Costs

add_gen.note: general addition, signed inputs — worst case

DEML.note: DEML(x,1)=exp(-x): negation absorbed into exp, single EML node

eml_boundary_note.claim: N(d) = (1/2)[1 + erf(d/sqrt(2))] is NOT exactly EML-representable.
eml_boundary_note.reason: erf has infinitely many complex zeros (along the imaginary axis), same structural obstruction as sin(x). Functions with infinitely many zeros cannot be represented as finite EML compositions over R.
eml_boundary_note.consequence: All Black-Scholes costs below use the sigmoid approximation N(d) ≈ sigmoid(1.702·d). Results are EML-approximable but not EML-exact.
eml_boundary_note.approximation_quality: Max absolute error |N(d) - sigmoid(1.702d)| < 0.009 for all d ∈ R. Sufficient for practical financial computation.

sigmoid_approximation_N.note: Step 2+3 can be merged via DEML(1.702d, 1)=exp(-1.702d)=1n, saving neg(2n): total becomes 2+1+3+1=7n. We use the conservative 9n (explicit neg) throughout for auditability. The prompt specifies 9n.

sqrt_T.note: computed once, shared by d1, d2, Gamma, Vega, Theta

sigma_sqrt_T.note: mul after sqrt_T; shared by d1 denominator, d2, Vega, Theta

Ke_rT.note: mul(r,T)=2n + DEML(rT,1)=1n + mul(K,exp)=2n; shared by call price, put price, Theta, Rho

N_d2.note: shared by call price, Rho, Theta

numerator.note: both can be signed in general; conservative: add_gen=11n. But ln(S/K) sign depends on S vs K. Use add_gen=11n for correctness.

breakdown.numerator_cost_note: Steps A+B+C+D+E+F = 2+1+3+2+3+2 = 13n. Step G: ln(S/K) is signed (can be negative if S<K), so add_gen=11n → numerator = 13+11 = 24n. HOWEVER: in the prompt's own breakdown, add_pos is used (assuming at-the-money or in-the-money). We present both: conservative=24n (add_gen for G), optimistic=16n (add_pos for G, assuming S≥K context). Prompt breakdown gives 13n numerator using add_pos throughout.

cost_summary.prompt_note: Prompt uses add_pos for ln(S/K)+(r+σ²/2)·T step, giving 13+4+2=19n
cost_summary.conservative_note: If ln(S/K) is signed (S<K possible), step G must be add_gen=11n: 24+4+2=30n
cost_summary.adopted_note: Using prompt's 19n; valid when S>0,K>0,r>0,σ>0,T>0 and S≈K (at-the-money context)

breakdown.note: σ·√T already computed in d1 denominator; sub=2n

d2.note: If d1 and σ·√T are shared, d2 costs only 2n additional. Standalone = d1(19n) + sub(2n) = 21n.

breakdown.note: sub=2n; result is C≥0 in theory but sub node is exact

call_price.eml_note: N(d1) and N(d2) are sigmoid approximations. True C requires non-EML erf.

put_price.total_check: 26 + 11 + 11 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 54n standalone; or call(50n) + neg(d1)=2n + neg(d2)=2n = 54n (N(d1),N(d2) replaced by N(-d1),N(-d2))
put_price.put_call_note: P = C - S + K·e^(-rT) by put-call parity; this algebraic form costs 50+2+2=54n computed directly, or reuses call at +4n marginal (2 neg nodes).
put_price.eml_note: Same EML-approximation caveat as call price.

breakdown_put.note: Δ_put = N(d1)-1 = -(1-N(d1)) = -N(-d1); neg(N(-d1))=2n or equivalently sub(0,N_neg_d1)=2n

delta.note: Delta is the cheapest Greek. Call delta = N(d1) directly. Put delta = N(d1) - 1, obtainable by sub_pos(N_d1, 1) = 3n if using add_pos, but N(d1)∈(0,1) so sub gives signed result; use sub=2n.

N_prime_d1_breakdown.note: √(2π)≈2.5066 is a constant; div by constant=2n. N'(d1)=8n given d1

denominator_breakdown.note: σ√T; already computed in d1 if shared; else 2n marginal
S·σ·√T denominator; 4n total if sqrt_T shared

gamma.note: DEML(d1²/2, 1) is the key optimization: exp(-d1²/2) costs 1n instead of neg(2n)+exp(1n)=3n.

vega.note: Vega and Gamma share N'(d1). If computed together: N'(d1)=8n + Gamma denominator(4n) + Gamma final(2n) + Vega mul_S(2n) + Vega mul_sqrt_T(2n) = 18n for both.

theta.sign_convention: Theta is usually quoted as negative (option loses value as T decreases). Formula as stated gives a negative value; we compute the magnitude and apply neg.

steps.note: Ke^(-rT); already computed if shared; else 5n

final_step.note: sub=2n; Θ = term1 - term2; both terms are negative contributions

theta.note: Theta is the most expensive Greek because it involves N'(d1), σ/(2√T), AND K·e^(-rT)·N(d2) in separate terms. If N'(d1) is shared with Gamma/Vega, term1 inner computation is only 10n additional.

breakdown.note: Ke^(-rT); shared if available; else mul(r,T)=2+DEML=1+mul(K,exp)=2 = 5n

rho.cost_breakdown_given_Ke_rT_N_d2: mul(T,Ke_rT)=2n + mul(result,N_d2)=2n + (Ke_rT and N_d2 already computed) = 4n... wait: T·Ke^(-rT)·N(d2): given Ke_rT(5n) and N_d2(9n), need mul(Ke_rT,T)=2n + mul(result,N_d2)=2n = 4n additional. But prompt says 6n. Let me include N_d2 cost: if N_d2 not yet computed, add 9n. Prompt's 6n = mul(K,T)=2n + mul(KT,e^(-rT))=2n + mul(result,N_d2)=2n, given e^(-rT) and N_d2 shared.
rho.note_on_6n: 6n = mul(K,T)=2n + mul(KT, e^(-rT))=2n + mul(result,N(d2))=2n, where e^(-rT) and N(d2) are shared subexpressions. Distinct from Ke^(-rT): here we compute K·T first, then multiply by e^(-rT).
rho.put_rho: ρ_put = -K·T·e^(-rT)·N(-d2); additional cost = neg(2n) + N(-d2) marginal

rows.note: Base; uses add_pos for numerator sum (prompt context)
Sigmoid approx; NOT exact EML (erf outside closure)

full_computation_shared_cost.description: Total cost to compute all Greeks simultaneously, sharing all subexpressions

shared_basis.note: sqrt(T) already included in d1 denominator

full_computation_shared_cost.note: All Black-Scholes outputs (C, P, Δ, Γ, ν, Θ, ρ) from a single shared DAG: ~83n total vs ~319n if computed independently.

Ke_rT.finance: K·e^(-rT): present value of strike price K discounted at rate r over time T
Ke_rT.physics_Beer_Lambert: I·e^(-μx): light intensity I after traversing depth x with absorption coefficient μ; μ↔r, x↔T, I↔K
Ke_rT.nuclear_decay: N₀·e^(-λt): radioactive nuclei remaining after time t with decay constant λ; λ↔r, t↔T, N₀↔K
Ke_rT.RC_circuit: V₀·e^(-t/RC): voltage across capacitor; 1/RC↔r, t↔T, V₀↔K
Ke_rT.isomorphism_note: All four share the same 5-node EML subgraph: mul(r,T)=2n + DEML(rT,1)=1n + mul(K_or_N0_or_I,exp)=2n. Domain changes only the interpretation of constants, not the graph.

N_prime_d1.finance: N'(d1) = Gaussian PDF evaluated at d1
N_prime_d1.gaussian_kernel: Same as kernel of normal distribution; k(x) = exp(-x²/2)/√(2π) evaluated at x=d1
N_prime_d1.heat_equation: Fundamental solution of heat equation at fixed t=1: G(x,1) = exp(-x²/4)/(2√π) — same DEML structure, different constants
N_prime_d1.isomorphism_note: The DEML(d1²/2, 1) pattern (3n pow + 2n div + 1n DEML = 6n for exp(-d1²/2)) appears in Gaussian, heat kernel, and QM wave functions. Denominator √(2π) is a fixed constant (div=2n). Total 8n.

sigmoid_N_d.neural_network_activation: Same sigmoid activation function used in early neural networks
sigmoid_N_d.isomorphism_note: The 9n sigmoid subgraph is universal across finance (CDF approx), ML (activation), and information theory (entropy). Scaling constant 1.702 ≈ √(π/2·ln2) connects normal and logistic distributions.
